---
title: "Network and traits"
author: "Benoît Pichon, Ismaël Lajaaiti"
bibliography: ../references.bib
prefer-html: true
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    toc: true
comments:
    hypothesis: true
crossref:
  custom:
    - kind: float
      reference-prefix: Figure S
      space-before-numbering: false
      key: sfig
      latex-env: sfig
      latex-list-of-description: Supporting Figure
---

```{r}
#| output: false
#| echo: false
library(renv)
renv::load()
renv::restore()
renv::status()

library(here)
library(targets)
tar_config_set(
  store = here::here("_targets")
)
```

## Notes copied from typst document

Three different ways to define robustness:
- functional trait space
- static network
- dynamic secondary extinctions

What are the relationships between these different facets of robustness. It
makes sense to have a global view, in particular in plant-pollinator network,
where traits govern species interactions.

- *Question 1:* What is the link between the functional/phylogenetic and network
role of species in different systems? Does uniqueness and originality drive
network species' roles?
- *Question 2:* Is there a link between the contribution of species to the
functional space and the possible secondary extinction their lead to? Ismaël:
This question makes me think of Alice's work with Jeff. She studied functional
contribution as a dynamic role of the species in the community. Maybe we could
try to relate dynamical functional contribution to trait uniqueness/originality? 


Some steps that need to be done:

- Read the papers and redefine the questions depending on exactly what has been
done

- Think about some descriptors of species role in networks (degree, centrality
measures, contribution to nestedness or modularity, ...XXX)

- Think about uniqueness and originality using functional traits, and when
possible, phylogenetic data?

- Find good data to test that: Solieveres paper, other papers where there is the
interaction network (plant-pollinator, so we extend that in foodwebs?),
possibility to use Biodesert (this would include Nico Yoann and Fernando at
least then)
- Perform the analyses

## Notes Ismaël

- @Coux-etal-2016:
  - [Code](https://github.com/CamilleCoux/Ntw_FD_roles/tree/master) and 
  [data](https://figshare.com/articles/dataset/Plant_pollinator_pollen_transportation_network_traits_and_pollinator_abundances_/3154078)
  - PCoA is used to summarise traits, could investigate the role of each trait
  individually in explaining species position in the network.
